_comment: RUN CAMPAIGNS IN dontAsk MODE, NEVER --dangerously-skip-permissions (bypassPermissions makes allow AND deny inert). Role split per the kernel security model: KERNEL (main session) writes only .slots/, campaigns/, and git metadata; CANDIDATE AGENTS write only inside .worktrees/**; TASK PACKAGES (tasks/**), the kernel itself (kernel/**), protocol docs, and data are hard-denied — the human edits them in a normal-mode session. Compiled agent defs land in .claude/agents/ via the bootstrap compile step (run before the campaign session starts, or allow the one-time Write interactively). git push denied — campaign branches stay local. WebSearch/WebFetch granted at the harness level but stripped per-agent at compile time (task.yml permissions.agents_web). CAVEAT: allowed Bash (sed/echo redirects) can write any path regardless of mode — prompt discipline (main never authors) remains the primary guard; review hooks before installing a third-party task package, they run arbitrary shell at bootstrap.
